title: genista cinerea subsp. ramosissima
intro: Genista cinerea subsp. ramosissima, also known as the highly branched broom, is a plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae) and a sub-species of the broom (Genista cinerea). It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and southwestern Europe, characterized by its dense, thorny branches and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to various health benefits such as reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Genista cinerea subsp. ramosissima can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
